title: Vhonte Tervho
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Mandalorian |image=[[File:Mandalady.jpg]] |name=Vhonte Tervho |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]]{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Red]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' **''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Have you identified a place to hold Ko Sai while we persuade her to our way of thinking?''"
"''Plan is to get her offworld as soon as we can. I was thinking of Mandalore. Rav Bralor owes me one. So does Vhonte Tervho. There are still some ''Cuy'val Dar'' around.|[[Ordo Skirata|Ordo]] and [[Kal Skirata]]|}} '''Vhonte Tervho''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]], who lived during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] twilight [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. A veteran [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] and an experienced [[Hunting/Legends|huntress]], Tervho was recruited by Mandalorian [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|leader]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] to serve as a trainer of [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] for the Republic's [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] during its infancy on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. As a member of the ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'', Tervho took on the role of training [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]] for nearly a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]], training more than a hundred [[Clone cadet/Legends|young clones]] and turning them into [[Commando/Legends|commandos]]. When the Republic's [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Army/Legends|army]] was finally deployed against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid army]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], Tervho departed Kamino for the first time in close to ten years, having earned a wealth of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for her services. Her contract completed, Tervho returned to the Mandalorians' cultural [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], though she would again take up the role of clone trainer later in the war, instructing more than a thousand of the Grand Army's elite [[Clone Commander/Legends|clone commanders]] in weapons training. ==Biography== {{Quote|You're the best in your field—the best soldiers, tacticians, sappers, communicators, survival experts. I picked you personally because I want you to train the best commandos in the galaxy. You'll have everything you need, whatever you want, except one thing—home. This is a top-secret project. You'll not tell anyone where you're going and you'll not leave Kamino, ever. As far as your friends and family are concerned, you're already dead.|[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], recruiting Vhonte Tervho and the other members of the ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]''|''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]''}} Vhonte Tervho was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]], who lived during the final [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Trained in combat by Mandalorian [[Verd'goten|tradition]], Tervho became a skilled [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] and [[Hunting/Legends|huntress]]. She worked as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] for a [[Time/Legends|time]], on at least one occasion working together with a group of other Mandalorian soldiers. Tervho was known to have [[Death/Legends|hunted]] a large, horned, [[Color/Legends|white]]-[[Hair/Legends|furred]] [[Species/Legends|creature]] through nighttime [[Water/Legends|snow]]. [[File:Mand-Group_small.jpg|thumb|left|225px|Vhonte Tervho leads a band of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] in combat]] After being recruited by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]] to serve as the [[Clone template/Legends|template]] for an [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] soldiers to be born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]],''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' [[Limb/Legends|hand]]-picked a total of one hundred beings from across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to train the [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]] that would make up the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army's]] [[Special forces/Legends|special forces]]. Vhonte Tervho was selected by Fett and she accepted his offer, joining the ranks of seventy-five Mandalorians and twenty-five beings of other assorted backgrounds known collectively as the ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'', a term which meant "Those Who No Longer Exist" in the Mandalorian [[Language/Legends|language]] of [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']]. Like the other members of the ''Cuy'val Dar'', Tervho became a training [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]], in charge of a total of one hundred and four [[Clone cadet/Legends|young clones]] whom she trained to adulthood and efficiency over the course of close to ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]].''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'' She was also among a number of Mandalorian members of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' to join Jango Fett in the combat education of his young son, [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].{{SW|url=news/so-uncivilized-great-gunslingers-in-star-wars-part-1|series=So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} Due to the top-secret nature of the clone army project, neither Tervho nor any other member of the ''Cuy'val Dar'' were allowed to leave Kamino before they were deployed with the rest of the army against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|droid forces]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] on behalf of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], the first battle of the emerging [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. At some point in [[Time/Legends|time]], Tervho incurred a debt to her ''Cuy'val Dar'' colleague and fellow Mandalorian, [[Kal Skirata]]. With her contract to train the [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] completed after [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], Tervho departed Kamino with a wealth of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for services rendered, and like many of the ''Cuy'val Dar''{{'s}} Mandalorian members she traveled back to [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], the Mandalorians cultural [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] located in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy's]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' However, Tervho later returned to Kamino to again [[ARC program/Legends|train clone troops]], imparting her skills with dual [[DC-17]] [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] to more than a thousand up and coming [[Clone Commander/Legends|clone commanders]]. Later in the war, during his quest to halt the accelerated aging the Republic's [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] had been subjected to by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Clone Master/Legends|Clone Masters]], Kal Skirata kidnapped Kaminoan [[Chief Scientist/Legends|Chief Scientist]] [[Ko Sai/Legends|Ko Sai]], and considered calling in the favor Tervho owed to him to house Ko Sai on Mandalore, though he instead settled on taking the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] to his own Mandalore [[Kyrimorut/Legends|homestead]] with the aid of their fellow former ''Cuy'val Dar'' trainer, [[Rav Bralor]]. ==Personality and traits== A [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] female, Vhonte Tervho served the six tenets of the ''[[Resol'nare]]'', living her life as a Mandalorian. She was trained in the use of blasters, capable of wielding two [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]] in combat at the same time, and was known for her prowess using twin DC-17 sidearms. Tervho was an efficient hunter, able to bring down large prey on her own. Vhonte Tervho possessed red [[Hair/Legends|hair]], which she styled into long braids that she allowed to hang free from beneath her [[Armor/Legends|helmet]]. She was also known to make good on favors she owed to her associates, and cared little for fame or celebrity. ==Equipment== Vhonte Tervho wore red [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] decorated with black-colored trim over a grey [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]]. Her armor's gauntlets featured a number of accessories, among them a retractable [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] mounted on the left-hand gauntlet. She wore a brown, multi-pouch [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]], and a tattered blue [[Cape/Legends|cape]] that wrapped like a [[Scarf/Legends|scarf]] around her neck. Tervho possessed a pair of [[Silver/Legends|silver]]-colored blaster pistols which she wore in dual [[Holster/Legends|holsters]] strapped to her thighs, and owned a place to live on the planet Mandalore. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Vhonte_Tervho_Print_by_Hodges_Art.jpg|thumb|right|225px|An unofficial image depicting Vhonte Tervho without her helmet, drawn by the character's creator, Tom Hodges]] {{Quote|I have submitted her name as Vhonte Tervho (pronounced Fon'tee Ter'Fo). I created her, may as well be able to name her. Just hope it sticks.|[[Tom Hodges|Thomas Hodges]]|{{JCFCite|url=posts/23050380|board=Literature|thread=Mandalorian Culture Article by Karen Traviss: Insider #86|user=[[Tom Hodges|TDHArtist]]|date=2006-02-02|time=6:43 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304012943/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/mandalorian-culture-article-by-karen-traviss-insider-86.22964649/page-4}}}} First introduced to ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] via a pair of nameless pictures of a female Mandalorian in the article ''[[The Mandalorians: People and Culture]]'', featured in the [[Star Wars Insider 86|eighty-sixth]] issue of [[Star Wars Insider]] magazine. The article's artist, [[Tom Hodges|Thomas Hodges]], later unofficially identified the pictured character as Vhonte Tervho, pronounced "''Fon'tee Ter'Fo''." However, her name only truly entered canon when it was later mentioned in the novel ''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'', the third entry in the [[Republic Commando|''Republic Commando'' series]] by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]], published [[October 30]], [[2007]]. Tervho was later mentioned in a [[StarWars.com]] blog entry by [[Edward M. Erdelac|Ed Erdelac]] entitled "[[So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars]]," which referenced her contributions to the ''Cuy'val Dar'' while adding a connection with the character of Boba Fett. Tervho's visual appearance from the ''People and Culture'' Insider article appears to have served as the visual basis [[Entertainment Earth]]'s [[action figure]] of the separate Mandalorian character, [[Rav Bralor]]. No official in-universe connection has been made between the two individuals, though Mandalorians wearing similar armor are not unheard of. ==Appearances== *''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' {{1stID|Vhonte Tervho}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}} {{1stp}} {{Po}} *{{SW|url=news/so-uncivilized-great-gunslingers-in-star-wars-part-1|series=So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Vhonte Tervho}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |pl=Vhonte Tervho }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tervho, Vhonte}} [[Category:Cuy'val Dar|Tervho, Vhonte]] [[Category:Females|Tervho, Vhonte]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species|Tervho, Vhonte]] [[Category:Mandalorians|Tervho, Vhonte]]
